components.title: Explore the building
components.lead: Plan your visit with direct access to the building story, tours, maps, event rentals, and press coverage.
paragraphs: Use About for the building story, Art Tours for guided visits, Maps for directions, Rent Space for private events, and Press for coverage.

components.headline: Third Fridays return on Friday, April 17, 2026
components.body: Third Fridays bring more than 60 venues together for a free public evening from 5 to 9 pm, with exhibits, music, food, and vendors throughout the building.

components.title: Contact 78th Street Studios
paragraphs: Studios at 78th Street, LLC lists 78thstreetstudios@gmail.com and 1300 West 78th Street, Cleveland, Ohio 44102 in the shared footer.

metadata.description: Northeast Ohio's eclectic arts maze with galleries, studios, events, and industrial character.

components.headline: Northeast Ohio's Eclectic Arts Maze
components.subheadline: A 170,000-square-foot Cleveland landmark filled with galleries, artist studios, performance spaces, recording rooms, and year-round events.

components.alt: Featured gallery image from inside 78th Street Studios
components.caption: A glimpse of the galleries and long industrial corridors that define the building's atmosphere.

items.title: Art Galleries
items.body: More than sixty venues and creative businesses make the complex a destination for collectors, visitors, and working artists.

items.title: Working Studios
items.body: Artists, designers, photographers, and publishers work in the same industrial footprint that visitors tour each month.

items.title: Third Fridays
items.body: Free public viewing hours bring exhibits, ambient music, food, and pop-up vendors together across four floors.

items.title: Private Events
items.body: The same warehouse atmosphere that draws art crowds is also used for weddings, corporate gatherings, and special events.

components.headline: Host an event inside Cleveland's best-known arts maze
components.body: The homepage pairs its arts pitch with a venue-rental push for weddings, corporate events, and vendor opportunities inside the same industrial setting.

metadata.description: The history, monthly events, and cultural reach of 78th Street Studios in Cleveland.

components.headline: Built in 1905, still full of working creatives
components.subheadline: The former Baker Electric Motor Vehicle building later housed American Greetings' creative teams and now anchors one of Cleveland's most distinctive art destinations.

components.alt: Interior view of the historic warehouse at 78th Street Studios
components.caption: Old freight doors, long corridors, and raw industrial surfaces still shape the way the building feels.

components.title: 78th Street Studios map
components.caption: Use the embedded map to get directions to the building in Cleveland.

components.title: Why the building matters
components.lead: 78th Street Studios stands as the largest art and design complex in Northeast Ohio and a recurring public destination for monthly arts events.
paragraphs: Visitors can explore 170,000 square feet, more than 60 venues, and a mix of galleries, artist studios, performance rooms, music recording spaces, and creative businesses under one roof.

Third Fridays remain the clearest example of that energy, bringing a free monthly viewing night from 5 to 9 pm with exhibits, ambient music, cuisine, pop-up vendors, and a district-wide spillover into Gordon Square.

The structure itself predates that cultural role. Built in 1905 for the Baker Electric Motor Vehicle Company, it later became home to American Greetings' creative studios before its current life as a center for artists, designers, makers, and events.

That layered history explains why the place feels different from a typical gallery building. The old freight infrastructure, hardwood floors, and warehouse scale are still present, but they now frame a mix of contemporary studios, exhibitions, and public gatherings.

items.title: Art studios
items.body: The current directory highlights Allen Kradlak Studio, Hilary Gent Studio, Megan Frankenfield Studio, Studio Brennan, and other working artist spaces.

items.title: Art galleries
items.body: ARTneo Museum, Derek Hess Gallery, HEDGE Gallery, Kenneth Paul Lesko Gallery, and White Lotus Gallery are part of the building's gallery roster.

items.title: Design and conservation
items.body: Antonym Collective, Canopy Press, Fresh Eggs Design, Galloway Art Conservation, and Walken Frame & Art show how the directory extends beyond gallery walls.

items.title: Photo, film, and entertainment
items.body: Bounce Light Studios, Cleveland Film Factory, Bent Crayon Records, CAN Journal, and Talespinner Children's Theatre broaden the tenant mix.

components.title: A few facts visitors should know

items.title: Magazine and catalog shoots
items.body: The building has been used for Cleveland Magazine photography and for industrial-style Arhaus catalog work.

items.title: Printing and apparel roots
items.body: Jakprints and Strhess Clothing are both part of the building's longer creative-business history.

items.title: Recording history
items.body: The former Lava Room Recording space hosted projects connected to INXS, Creed, Papa Roach, Warrant, and The Pussycat Dolls.

items.title: Event history
items.body: smARTspace at 78th has hosted fan events, and the property is still used for private gatherings alongside public arts programming.

items.title: Publishing ties
items.body: Alternative Press remained in the building until late 2021, extending its role beyond galleries into music and media.

components.headline: Visit the building or follow the next event
components.body: Explore the tenant directory, maps, mailing list, and current public-event details before your next visit.
